schema.appDescription: Calculate future value of your property investment. Enter purchase price, annual appreciation rate, and holding period to see projected value.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the Property Appreciation Calculator.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time as you type.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown to see numbers over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust inputs to compare scenarios and optimise your decision.

faq.q: What is the average property appreciation rate in India?
faq.a: Historical real estate appreciation in India: metro cities (Mumbai, Bangalore, Delhi) 7–10% p.a. over 20 years. Tier 2 cities: 8–12% p.a. However, these are averages — specific locations can do much better or worse. Always factor in location micro-market trends.

faq.q: How accurate is property appreciation forecasting?
faq.a: Real estate appreciation is highly location-specific and cannot be accurately predicted. Factors: infrastructure development (metro stations, IT parks), population growth, regulatory changes (FSI/FAR changes), economic conditions. Use this calculator for scenario planning with optimistic/pessimistic assumptions.

faq.q: Is capital gains tax applicable on property appreciation?
faq.a: Yes. If you sell the property, LTCG (held >2 years) is taxed at 12.5% (Budget 2024 rate, no indexation) or 20% with indexation — whichever is lower for transactions before July 2024. Reinvesting in a new home (Section 54) or specified bonds (Section 54EC) can defer/reduce LTCG.
No. Calculations run in your browser.
Yes. Works on all modern smartphones.

examples.heading: ₹50 lakh property at 7% appreciation for 10 years
examples.text: ₹50 lakh at 7% p.a. for 10 years = ₹98.36 lakh. Capital gain: ₹48.36 lakh. LTCG tax at 12.5% = ₹6.05 lakh. Net gain after tax: ₹42.31 lakh. If reinvested in new property: tax deferred under Section 54.

examples.heading: Metro vs Tier 2 — appreciation difference
examples.text: ₹50 lakh at 7% (metro, 10 years) = ₹98.36 lakh. At 10% (Tier 2, 10 years) = ₹129.69 lakh. Difference of ₹31.33 lakh. But liquidity and rental yield in Tier 2 cities may be lower — higher appreciation, lower rental income.
